macro_segments.title: Introduction and the Mishna on Separating Challah in Tumah on Yom Tov

micro_segments.title: Opening remarks and Rosh Hashanah reference
The Mishna's three-way debate on separating challah from tamei dough on Yom Tov Pesach

macro_segments.title: The Debate between Rava and Rav Chisda on Cooking on Yom Tov for the Weekday

micro_segments.title: Introduction of the heated debate: Rava's position that you could eat it on Yom Tov (ho'il principle)
Rav Chisda's counter-position: intent matters, requiring specific Yom Tov purpose
How to cook from Yom Tov to Shabbos with Eruv Tavshilin

macro_segments.title: The Case of Lechem HaPanim (Showbread): When and How It Is Baked

micro_segments.title: Rava's challenge to Rav Chisda using the Braita about showbread
Calendar and counting: the nine, ten, and eleven day scenarios for Lechem HaPanim
The Rosh Hashanah scenario and the problem of eating on Yom Kippur
Resolution: how Rosh Hashanah is counted differently in Bayit HaMikdash times

macro_segments.title: Rav Chisda's Counterattack: Shabbos/Yom Tov vs. Distant Shabbos and the Rabbinic Category of Shvut

micro_segments.title: The Braita's rule that Lechem HaPanim cannot override Shabbos or Yom Tov
Rava's question: if you say tzorkhei Shabbat na'asin b'Yom Tov, why not cook for distant Shabbos?
Rav Chisda's answer: shvut krovah hitiru, shvut rechoka lo hitiru (rabbinic distinction)
Rabban Gamliel's lenient position: allowing even distant shvut
Explanation of the dispute: Rava's ho'il vs. Rav Chisda's license for nearby days

macro_segments.title: The Case of Shtei HaLechem (Two Loaves of Shavuos): When They Cannot Be Baked

micro_segments.title: Rav Mari's question from a Braita about the two loaves of Shavuos
Why Shtei HaLechem cannot be baked on Yom Tov: the issue of korban vs. personal food
Rashi's explanation: according to Rabba's ho'il principle, nothing can be theoretically eaten now
The response: you cannot bake for the sake of bringing a korban, only for personal purposes
Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel's position: permitting baking Shtei HaLechem on Shavuos (debate over korban purpose)

macro_segments.title: Rav Chisda's Challenge via Rav Acha: The Principle of Ho'il and Plowing on Yom Tov

micro_segments.title: Rav Chisda sends a challenge through Rav Acha bar Rav Huna to Rava
The Mishna in Makkos: eight prohibitions from making one furrow
The question: if ho'il applies to cooking, should you not get lashes for plowing?
The hypothetical use: covering chicken blood with plowed dirt

macro_segments.title: Attempts to Resolve the Plowing Question: Clods, Rocks, and Mud

micro_segments.title: Rav Papa bar Shmuel's answer: discussing hard clumps that cannot be used for covering blood
Back and forth: grinding clumps, rocks above and dirt below, and muddy soil
Final resolution: the mud is too thick to be useful for covering blood

macro_segments.title: Abaye's Question: Cooking Gid HaNasheh (Sciatic Nerve) in Milk on Yom Tov

micro_segments.title: The Braita: five lashes for cooking gid hanasheh in milk on Yom Tov
The question: if ho'il applies, should you not get lashes for making fire?
Response: you don't get lashes for fire because theoretically it has permitted uses

macro_segments.title: Switching the Braita Text and the Problem of Muktza Wood as a Violation

micro_segments.title: Proposal to switch the Braita: replace fire violation with using muktza wood
Objection: is muktza biblical? Response: yes, from the concept of preparation (Parashat Shishi)
Counter-objection: why does muktza prevent ho'il when there's no permitted use?

macro_segments.title: Is Muktza a Biblical Prohibition? Evidence from Korbanos and Yaakov's Question

micro_segments.title: Rav Chisda's original source: bringing a Tamid from a wild gazelle that was muktza
The principle from Yechezkel: korbanos must come from permitted (mutar be'achilah) sources
The distinction: muktza is not intrinsically forbidden (issur gufo) but forbidden by circumstance
Conclusion: if muktza were biblical, it should be treated like tevel, but it is not
The unresolved question: what biblical violation replaces the fire prohibition in the Braita?

topical_tags.term: Rabban Gamliel / Rabban Shimon ben Gamliel
tzorkhei Shabbat na'asin b'Yom Tov (Shabbos needs on Yom Tov)
